member.name: Dr Danny Chambers
member.synopsis: Dr Danny Chambers is the Liberal Democrat MP for Winchester , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Mental Health).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Strategic Defence Review
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of the time taken to progress the Transformation Programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defence Reform is the most significant transformation of Defence in over 50 years. Since April 2025, we have made substantial progress in reshaping the organisation including establishing clear accountabilities flowing from the four most senior leaders in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Sir John Moore Barracks: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what estimate he has made of the proportion of military accommodation in Sir John Moore Barracks and Worthy down that does not meet the Decent Homes Standard.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence remains committed to providing Service personnel and their families with high-quality, modern and well-maintained accommodation. As of 15 June 2026, there are 415 Service Family Accommodation (SFA) properties serving Sir John Moore...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Further Education: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made of the costs to FE colleges of their ineligibility for the VAT refund scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While VAT is a matter for His Majesty’s Treasury, the department recognises that the VAT status of further education (FE) providers remains an issue of interest to the sector. Analysis indicates that VAT receipts for FE colleges are around £200 million pe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Winchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate she has made of the number of police officers assigned to Winchester constituency between 2016 and 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office publishes data twice a year on the size and composition of the police workforce, in the ‘Police Workforce, England and Wales’ statistical bulletin, available here: https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/police-workforce-england-and-wale...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Hampshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what proportion of police officers in Hampshire are classed as rural officers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office does not hold information on the number of ‘rural police officers’.The Home Office collects and publishes data biannually on the size and composition of the police workforce, in the ‘Police Workforce, England and Wales’ statistical bulleti...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Planning: Hampshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of the new planning process laid out entitled ‘Roll out of the new plan-making process’ guidance updated on March 6 with the plan for
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has made clear that plan-making should commence in the new system in line with the timings laid out in the regulations that came into force on 25 March. We recognise the challenges posed to plan making by local authority re-organisation and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Antimicrobials: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what his planned timetable is for the launch of the UK antimicrobial subscription model, including (a) the award and (b) commencement of contracts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On behalf of all the nations of the United Kingdom, NHS England published an invitation to tender for the UK subscription model for antimicrobials on 24 August 2024. The assessment of each product is being undertaken by a panel convened by the National In...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Antimicrobials: Prescriptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Neighbourhood Health Framework published on 17 March, what assessment has been made of the effectiveness of the inclusion of infection diagnostics within the plan; and what st
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Neighbourhood Health Framework focuses on improving access to diagnostic services by bringing them closer to home as part of the wider ambition to shift care from hospital to community settings. In this context, it commits NHS England to reviewing dir...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many shifts were recorded as not fully-staffed on England’s maternity units in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department cannot provide information on how many shifts were recorded as not fully staffed on England’s maternity units in each of the last five years, as this information is held regionally by the National Health Service and by individual NHS trusts...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Swimming Pools: Closures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, how many local authority swimming pools have closed in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of ensuring public access to swimming and leisure facilities. Sport England, our Arm’s Length Body for grassroots sport, monitors facilities stock through their Active Places dataset. As of March 2026, 308 local au...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Dogs: Animal Breeding
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether the Animal Welfare Strategy consultation on dog breeding will include (a) brachycephalic dogs and (b) extreme conformities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the Animal Welfare Strategy, the Government has committed to launch a consultation on dog breeding reform to improve the health and welfare of breeding dogs and their puppies. This will include proposals to improve the health and welfare standa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Midwives: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many undergraduates enrolled for a midwifery degree in each of the last 10 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data published by the Office for Students in the Higher Education Students Early Statistics Survey (HESES) collates figures submitted by individual higher education providers to give an indication of the number of students studying in each academic year.H...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Midwives
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many midwives started working in the NHS in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England publishes Hospital and Community Health Services workforce statistics for England. This covers staff working for hospital trusts and integrated care in England. This data is drawn from the Electronic Staff Record, the human resources system fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Noise
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps the Government is taking to address noise pollution, reduce noise poverty and the harm to physical and mental health.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to ensuring that noise is managed effectively to promote good health and minimise disruption to people’s quality of life. Defra manages noise through the Environmental Noise (England) Regulations 2006. The Regulations require ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Noise
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what plans the Government has to implement the recommendations of the 2023 House of Lords report on noise.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to ensuring that noise is managed effectively to promote good health and minimise disruption to people’s quality of life. Defra manages noise through the Environmental Noise (England) Regulations 2006. The Regulations require ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Occupational Therapy: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that occupational therapists receive adequate training opportunities and support.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England recognises the vital role occupational therapists play across the health and care system and is committed to supporting a skilled, sustainable workforce.Post registration training and development for occupational therapists is the responsibili...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Ehlers-Danlos Support UK
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will commit to meeting with EDS UK before the end of the year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Rt Hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, and ministers regularly meet with a wide range of stakeholders, including patient groups and charities, to inform policy development and to understand lived experience. Officials from t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how much financial support she has provided to Hampshire County Council specifically for pothole repairs in the last 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: During financial year 2025/26, the Government provided approximately £1.6 billion to support local highways authorities across England, a £500 million uplift compared to the previous year. As part of this, Hampshire County Council received a total of over...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Graduates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what recent estimate he has made of the level of decrease in graduate job levels due to artificial intelligence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to growth, and a key pillar of this is ensuring that everyone, including graduates, have access to good, meaningful work. As part of our ongoing assessment of labour market trends, we note that job vacancies have declined fr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Graduates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of AI on graduate job opportunities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to growth, and a key pillar of this is ensuring that everyone, including graduates, have access to good, meaningful work. As part of our ongoing assessment of labour market trends, we note that job vacancies have declined fr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions
